task_completion.description: Open Wondershare EdrawMax and create a new document
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Wondershare EdrawMax application window is visible with a blank or active canvas.

task_completion.description: Add core network components to Page 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: Page 1 of the document contains shapes representing a perimeter firewall, a core router (indicating ISP connectivity), and a core distribution layer switch.

task_completion.description: Add access layer and endpoint components to Page 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: Page 1 includes two access layer switches, a server farm zone with three server nodes (file, web, DNS/DHCP), four end-user devices, and two wireless access points.

task_completion.description: Connect devices and apply a professional color theme
task_completion.visual_evidence: The devices on Page 1 are connected with lines representing cabling or trunk links, and the diagram uses a cohesive, professional color theme.

task_completion.description: Create a structured IP Addressing Plan on Page 2
task_completion.visual_evidence: Page 2 of the document contains text, a table, or structured data detailing subnets, IP ranges, VLAN IDs, and device roles.

task_completion.description: Save the document to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or terminal window shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/campus_network_topology' (or with the .eddx extension).

integrity.description: Agent used the EdrawMax GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with EdrawMax menus, shape libraries, and canvas, rather than generating the file via a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Diagram consists of actual EdrawMax shapes and connections
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram elements appear as individual, editable shapes and connectors from the EdrawMax library, not a single imported static image.

integrity.description: The IP Addressing Plan is genuinely formatted
integrity.visual_evidence: The IP plan on Page 2 is structured (e.g., using tables, aligned text, or specific formatting tools in EdrawMax) rather than a raw copy-paste of the prompt text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/campus_network_topology.eddx. The document must have at least 2 pages. Page 1 must contain network shapes including a firewall and layered switching components. Page 2 must contain an IP addressing plan.
